translated_name: The Chapter on the Way to the Beyond
blurb: This is perhaps the most sophisticated and moving poetic composition in early Buddhism. It begins with a brahmin, suffering an unwanted curse, who sends his students to seek the Buddha. At the end of a long journey they ask questions, the nature of which indicate their subtle philosophy and deep meditation. It concludes with an emotional declaration of faith and determination.

children.blurb: A brahman teacher sends his students to the Buddha to see if the latter is truly awakened.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Ajita
children.blurb: A brahman questions the Buddha about mindfulness, discernment, and the cessation of name-and-form.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Tissametteyya
children.blurb: Who in the world is truly contented, truly free, truly a great person?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Puṇṇaka
children.blurb: Birth and aging can be overcome, not through sacrificial rituals, but through training the mind to go beyond perturbation.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Mettagū
children.blurb: How does one cross the flood of birth and old age, sorrow and grief?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Dhotaka
children.blurb: How can one become freed of all doubt?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Upasīva
children.blurb: What support should one hold on to in order to cross over the flood of craving? Can an awakened person be described?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Nanda
children.blurb: Who deserves to be called a sage? Who has crossed over birth and aging?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Hemaka
children.blurb: How do you cross over entanglements in the world?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Todeyya
children.blurb: How to recognize an emancipated person.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Kappa
children.blurb: What is the island above the flood of the great danger of birth?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Jatukaṇṇī
children.blurb: How does one abandon birth and aging?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Bhadrāvudha
children.blurb: Bhadrāvudha asks the Buddha: How did you come to know the Dhamma?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Udaya
children.blurb: How to reach unbinding and bring consciousness to a halt.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Posala
children.blurb: How to develop insight after mastering the perception of nothingness.

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Mogharāja
children.blurb: How should one view the world so as to escape the king of Death?

children.translated_name: The Questions of the Student Piṅgiya
children.blurb: Alarmed by the deterioration of his aging body, Piṅgiya asks the Buddha how to conquer birth and decay.

children.translated_name: Homage to the Way to the Beyond
children.blurb: The questions and events are summarized.

children.translated_name: Preserving the Way to the Beyond
children.blurb: Piṅgiya, after becoming a non-returner, explains to his former teacher his devotion to the Buddha.
